strategic_profile.executive_summary: Western Michigan University (WMU) is a dynamic, learner-centered public research university classified as an R2 institution. Offering over 140 undergraduate majors, it pairs the resources of a large research university with experience-driven learning, highlighted by standout programs in aviation, engineering, business, and fine arts.

analyst_takeaways.insight: High overall acceptance rate of 84.6%, but highly selective direct-admit pathways exist for capped programs like Aviation Flight Science and Nursing.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Applicants to standard programs have high admission certainty, but those targeting premier/impacted majors must apply early and demonstrate strong academic profiles.

analyst_takeaways.insight: WMU's Medallion Scholarship is one of the most lucrative merit awards at public institutions, valued at over $93,000 across four years.
analyst_takeaways.implication: High-achieving applicants should ensure they meet the December 15 Early Action deadline to automatically compete for this transformative financial package.

analyst_takeaways.insight: WMU utilizes a flat-rate tuition model for 12-15 credits, though differential tuition applies to programs in Business, Engineering, and Fine Arts.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Students in these pre-professional tracks should budget for higher-than-base tuition costs.

analyst_takeaways.insight: The campus supports a vibrant student life with over 400 organizations, Greek life, and Division I athletics in the Mid-American Conference (MAC).
analyst_takeaways.implication: Students looking for a traditional, spirited state-school environment will find it here, with strong regional networking connections.

campus_dynamics.social_environment: Vibrant and traditional large-campus feel with NCAA Division I sports, active Greek life, and popular campus events like Bronco Bash.
campus_dynamics.transportation_impact: Suburban/urban blend with a dedicated campus bus system. Many students bring cars, though parking can sometimes be an issue.
campus_dynamics.research_impact: As an R2 university, undergraduate research is heavily encouraged. Specific stipends, such as the $3,000 engagement grant attached to the Medallion Scholarship, actively fund undergraduate research and study abroad.

current_status.test_policy_details: Test-optional for both admission and merit scholarship consideration. If submitted, WMU superscores both the SAT and ACT. Test scores are self-reported during the application phase.

longitudinal_trends.notes: WMU does not utilize a waitlist for general admissions.

gpa.notes: Average GPA reported by incoming freshman class.

testing.policy_note: WMU has permanently adopted a test-optional policy for both admissions and scholarships.

colleges.name: College of Aviation
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Ideal for students pursuing commercial piloting or aviation management. Must apply Early Action (Dec 15) for fall entry consideration due to strict capacity limits.
colleges.student_archetype: Driven, technically minded future pilots and aerospace professionals.

majors.name: Aviation Flight Science
majors.special_requirements: Strict fall entry only; freshmen must apply by Dec 15.
majors.notes: One of the most renowned and selective aviation programs in the Midwest.

colleges.name: College of Health and Human Services
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Strong fit for future healthcare workers, particularly in nursing, occupational therapy, and social work.

majors.name: Nursing (Bronson School of Nursing)
majors.special_requirements: First-year students seeking direct admission must apply for fall entry by December 15.

colleges.name: College of Engineering and Applied Sciences
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Best for students targeting civil, mechanical, or computer science careers. Offers an accelerated master's track.

majors.name: Computer Science
majors.special_requirements: Requires owning a laptop meeting specific department specs.

core_courses: CS 4310 Design and Analysis of Algorithms
electives: CS 2230 Computer Organization and Assembly Language
CS 4800 Theory of Computation I: Automata

majors.notes: CAC/ABET accredited program. Requires 30 credit hours of math, statistics, and lab sciences.

colleges.name: Haworth College of Business
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Great for corporate-minded students. Known for heavy integration with local employers and the Zhang Career Center.
colleges.student_archetype: Career-oriented, communicative future managers and marketers.

majors.name: Marketing
majors.notes: Consistently cited as one of WMU's most popular degree programs.

application_deadlines.notes: Non-binding. Required for consideration for Medallion Scholarship, priority Lee Honors College invitation, and competitive programs (Nursing, Aviation).
Rolling Admission deadline for most general academic programs.

supplemental_requirements.details: Up to 60 Medallion Scholarship semifinalists are invited to participate in interviews.

holistic_factors.specific_differentiators: General admissions is highly numbers-driven and does not require essays or recommendation letters for the majority of first-year applicants.

major_selection_tactics: Apply early to competitive programs like Aviation Flight Science and Direct Admit Nursing, which strictly cut off considerations after the Dec 15 EA deadline.
college_ranking_tactics: Apply by December 15 to automatically be considered for the Medallion Scholarship, which requires no extra application but is highly competitive.
application_strategy.alternate_major_strategy: For applicants targeting capped programs like Aviation or Nursing, list a less impacted alternate major, as the general university has a much higher acceptance rate than these specific schools.

financials.tuition_model: Flat-rate tuition covering 12-15 credits per semester. Differential tuition surcharges apply for upper-level courses and specific colleges (Haworth College of Business, Engineering and Applied Sciences, Fine Arts).

scholarships.name: Medallion Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: Covers $16,000/yr for tuition, standard room costs, and provides a one-time $3,000 engagement grant for study abroad/research.

scholarships.name: Global Education Merit Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: Available to international degree-seeking undergraduate students. Requires a 3.50 minimum GPA.

scholarships.name: WMU Transfer Academic Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: $1,500 per year for incoming transfer students holding a 3.5 GPA or above.

scholarships.name: WMU Academic Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: Available to currently enrolled students maintaining a 3.50+ GPA.

scholarships.name: Paper Technology Foundation Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: Awarded to incoming paper engineering students; can be stacked with other WMU merit scholarships.

ap_policy.general_rule: Awards credit for most AP exams with a score of 3 or higher.
exceptions: Physics requires a score of 4 or 5.
ap_policy.usage: Credits are applied to the student's record after the start of their first semester; counts toward degree requirements and early graduation.

ib_policy.general_rule: Awards credit for Higher Level (HL) exam scores of 5 or higher. Standard Level (SL) exams do not receive credit.

transfer_articulation.restrictions: Transfer courses are accepted for credit only (no GPA transfer). Requires a minimum 'C' (2.0) grade to be eligible.

what_it_takes: A solid high school GPA (around 3.5) with mostly As and Bs.
Applying early (by Dec 15) to unlock the highest scholarship tiers and program availability.
For elite programs (Nursing/Aviation), rigor in math and science courses is highly recommended.
common_activities: Attending Division I MAC sporting events
essay_tips: General admissions do not require an essay.
Focus heavily on academic metrics rather than narrative for initial entry.
red_flags: Applying after December 15 if aiming for Nursing or Aviation.
A grade trend sloping downward in the junior/senior year.
insights: Campus offers a great mix of big-school energy with smaller department communities, especially within the Lee Honors College.
Business and engineering students enjoy robust career placement resources like the Zhang Career Center.
Students appreciate the updated facilities and buildings on campus, though intro-level courses are often taught in large lecture halls.
